CLASS EXERCISE : BUILDING A RAG PIPLINE BASIS TREDENCE DATA

In [1]:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

# Send a request to the Tredence website
response = requests.get(
    url="https://www.tredence.com/",
)
# Parse the HTML content
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')

# Attempt to find the main content
# Option 1: Try to find the HTML5 <main> tag
main_content_element = soup.find('main')

# Option 2: If <main> is not found, try to find all paragraph tags (<p>)
if not main_content_element:
    paragraphs = soup.find_all('p')
    if paragraphs:
        # Join text from all paragraphs, adding a new line between them
        extracted_text = "\n\n".join([p.get_text(separator=' ', strip=True) for p in paragraphs])
        print(extracted_text)
    else:
        print("No main content found within a <main> tag or any paragraph text.")
        print("You may need to inspect the page manually to find the specific ID or class for the content you want.")
else:
    # If <main> content is found, extract its text
    print(main_content_element.get_text(separator=' ', strip=True))

Integrated data management for operationalizing data insights to help you provide the right care, at the right time, in the right way.

text_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(
    chunk_size=512,   # Set chunk size to 512 characters

Step 1: store the scraped text for this single URL

In [6]:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

url = "https://www.tredence.com/"

response = requests.get(url)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, "html.parser")

main_content_element = soup.find("main")

if main_content_element:
    extracted_text = main_content_element.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True)
else:
    paragraphs = soup.find_all("p")
    if paragraphs:
        extracted_text = "\n\n".join(
            [p.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) for p in paragraphs]
        )
    else:
        extracted_text = ""

# Store into a small corpus DataFrame
docs_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "url": url,
            "text": extracted_text
        }
    ]
)

docs_df.head()

url text
0 https://www.tredence.com/ Move ideas from\r\n ...

Step 2 cell: chunk the page text into overlapping segments

In [8]:
import textwrap

# Parameters you can tune
CHUNK_SIZE = 1000    # characters per chunk
CHUNK_OVERLAP = 100  # overlapping characters between chunks

def chunk_text(text, chunk_size=CHUNK_SIZE, chunk_overlap=CHUNK_OVERLAP):
    """
    Simple character-based chunking with overlap.
    """
    if not text:
        return []
    
    chunks = []
    start = 0
    text_length = len(text)
    
    while start < text_length:
        end = start + chunk_size
        chunk = text[start:end]
        chunks.append(chunk)
        
        # Move start forward but keep some overlap
        start += chunk_size - chunk_overlap
    
    return chunks

# Apply chunking to the single row in docs_df
page_text = docs_df.loc[0, "text"]
chunks = chunk_text(page_text)

print(f"Number of chunks: {len(chunks)}")
print(f"First chunk preview:\n{chunks[0][:500]}")

Number of chunks: 16
First chunk preview:
Move ideas from
                                                        experimentation to
                                                        reality with
                                                        our accelerators.

Move ideas from experimentation
                                                        to reality with
                                                        our accelerators.

Move ideas from
                                                        experime

Step 3 cell: embeddings + Chroma storage

In [ ]:
# If not already installed in the environment, run this once:
# (You can comment this out after successful installation.)
!pip install -q chromadb openai

import os
import chromadb
from chromadb import PersistentClient
from openai import OpenAI

# Set your OpenAI API key (or rely on environment variable)
os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"] = ""  # replace with your key or remove if already set

# OpenAI client
client = OpenAI()

# Initialize Chroma persistent client (new-style API)
chroma_client = PersistentClient(path="./tredence_rag_db")

# Create or get a collection
collection = chroma_client.get_or_create_collection(name="tredence_website")

def embed_texts(texts):
    """
    Use OpenAI embeddings for a list of strings.
    """
    response = client.embeddings.create(
        model="text-embedding-3-small",  # choose embedding model as needed
        input=texts
    )
    return [item.embedding for item in response.data]

# Create IDs and metadata for chunks
chunk_ids = [f"tredence_chunk_{i}" for i in range(len(chunks))]
metadatas = [
    {
        "url": docs_df.loc[0, "url"],
        "chunk_index": i
    }
    for i in range(len(chunks))
]

# Compute embeddings for all chunks
chunk_embeddings = embed_texts(chunks)

# Add data to Chroma collection
collection.add(
    ids=chunk_ids,
    metadatas=metadatas,
    documents=chunks,
    embeddings=chunk_embeddings
)

print(f"Stored {len(chunks)} chunks in Chroma collection 'tredence_website'.")
print(f"Collection document count: {collection.count()}")

Stored 16 chunks in Chroma collection 'tredence_website'.
Collection document count: 16

Step 4 cell: query Chroma and answer with OpenAI

In [10]:
TOP_K = 3  # number of chunks to retrieve

def retrieve_relevant_chunks(question, top_k=TOP_K):
    """
    Embed the question and query Chroma for the most similar chunks.
    """
    # Embed the question
    question_embedding = embed_texts([question])[0]
    
    # Query Chroma
    results = collection.query(
        query_embeddings=[question_embedding],
        n_results=top_k,
        include=["documents", "metadatas", "distances"]
    )
    
    # Chroma returns lists; flatten them into a more convenient structure
    docs = results["documents"][0]
    metadatas = results["metadatas"][0]
    distances = results["distances"][0]
    
    retrieved = []
    for doc, meta, dist in zip(docs, metadatas, distances):
        retrieved.append({
            "text": doc,
            "metadata": meta,
            "distance": dist
        })
    return retrieved

def build_context(retrieved_chunks):
    """
    Concatenate retrieved chunks into a single context string.
    """
    context_parts = []
    for i, item in enumerate(retrieved_chunks):
        context_parts.append(
            f"Chunk {i} (distance={item['distance']:.4f}):\n{item['text']}\n"
        )
    return "\n\n".join(context_parts)

def answer_question(question):
    """
    Full RAG-style pipeline: retrieve context, then ask OpenAI to answer.
    """
    retrieved = retrieve_relevant_chunks(question, top_k=TOP_K)
    
    if not retrieved:
        return "No relevant content found in the index."
    
    context = build_context(retrieved)
    
    system_prompt = (
        "You are a helpful assistant answering questions based ONLY on the "
        "provided website content. If the answer is not in the context, say "
        "you don't know and suggest the user check the website."
    )
    
    user_prompt = (
        f"Question: {question}\n\n"
        f"Context from the Tredence website:\n{context}\n\n"
        "Answer the question using only this context. "
        "Be concise but complete."
    )
    
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",  # or another chat model
        messages=[
            {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
            {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}
        ]
    )
    
    return response.choices[0].message.content

# Quick manual test
test_question = "What services does Tredence provide?"
print(answer_question(test_question))

Tredence provides a range of services including:

- Agentic AI
- Generative AI
- Supply Chain Management
- Data Engineering
- Data Modernization
- Customer Experience (CX) Analytics
- MLOps and LLMOps
- Data Science
- Digital Engineering
- Supply Chain Control Tower
- Test and Learn Platform (TALP)
- On-shelf Availability (OSA)
- Customer Cosmos

They focus on actionable insights, predictive and prescriptive analytics, and enhancing customer experiences through personalization and real-time intelligence.
In [11]:
test_question = "What is tredence?"
print(answer_question(test_question))

Tredence is a company recognized as a leader in various analytics and consulting services, including generative AI, data engineering, and data science. It has received numerous accolades, such as being named a Great Place to Work® and appearing in the Inc. 5000 list for ten consecutive years. Additionally, Tredence focuses on modernizing business processes through intelligent agents and delivers insights that bridge the gap between data and value realization across various industries, including retail, banking, and healthcare.

Step 5 cell: Gradio chatbot UI over your RAG pipeline

In [ ]:
!pip install -q gradio

import gradio as gr

def rag_chatbot(message, history):
    """
    Minimal Gradio chat function.
    `message`: latest user message (string).
    `history`: previous chat turns (Gradio-managed, we don't need to modify it).
    We just return the bot reply; Gradio handles appending to history.
    """
    if not message or message.strip() == "":
        reply = "Please ask a question about the Tredence website."
    else:
        reply = answer_question(message)
    
    # Return bot's reply only; ChatInterface will update history internally.
    return reply

demo = gr.ChatInterface(
    fn=rag_chatbot,
    title="Tredence Website Q&A",
    description="Ask questions about the Tredence homepage (RAG over scraped content)."
)

demo.launch()

* Running on local URL:  http://127.0.0.1:7860
* To create a public link, set `share=True` in `launch()`.

Visualization cell: UMAP + colored corpus + retrieval inspection


















In [13]:






# INSTALL DEPENDENCIES (run once)
!pip install -q umap-learn scikit-learn

import numpy as np
import umap
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# 1. FETCH ALL EMBEDDINGS, METADATA, DOCUMENTS FROM CHROMA

# 'ids' are always returned; do NOT include them explicitly.
all_data = collection.get(include=["embeddings", "metadatas", "documents"])

all_embeddings = np.array(all_data["embeddings"])
all_ids = all_data["ids"]
all_metadatas = all_data["metadatas"]
all_docs = all_data["documents"]

# Build a DataFrame for inspection and coloring
corpus_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "id": all_ids,
    "embedding_idx": np.arange(len(all_ids)),
    "metadata": all_metadatas,
    "text": all_docs
})

# Extract chunk_index from metadata (if present)
def get_chunk_index(meta):
    if isinstance(meta, dict) and "chunk_index" in meta:
        return meta["chunk_index"]
    return -1  # fallback if not present

corpus_df["chunk_index"] = corpus_df["metadata"].apply(get_chunk_index)

# Bucket chunk_index for coloring (0–4, 5–9, etc.)
corpus_df["chunk_bucket"] = (corpus_df["chunk_index"] // 5).astype(int)

# 2. FIT UMAP ON FULL EMBEDDING SET

umap_transform = umap.UMAP(
    n_neighbors=15,
    min_dist=0.1,
    metric="cosine",
    random_state=0,
    transform_seed=0
).fit(all_embeddings)

# 3. VISUALIZATION FUNCTION

def visualize_query(question, n_results=TOP_K):
    """
    Visualize where a question and its retrieved chunks lie in the embedding space.
    """
    # a) Use the same retrieval logic from your RAG pipeline
    retrieved = retrieve_relevant_chunks(question, top_k=n_results)
    
    # b) Embed query and query Chroma to get embeddings of retrieved chunks
    q_emb = embed_texts([question])[0]
    q_results = collection.query(
        query_embeddings=[q_emb],
        n_results=n_results,
        include=["embeddings"]  # <--- FIX: no 'ids' here
    )
    # ids are returned regardless of `include`
    retrieved_ids = q_results["ids"][0]
    retrieved_embeddings = np.array(q_results["embeddings"][0])
    
    # c) Project all embeddings
    projected_all = umap_transform.transform(all_embeddings)
    
    # d) Project retrieved embeddings
    projected_retrieved = umap_transform.transform(retrieved_embeddings)
    
    # e) Project query embedding
    projected_query = umap_transform.transform(np.array([q_emb]))
    
    # f) Mask for retrieved points in the corpus
    retrieved_mask = corpus_df["id"].isin(retrieved_ids).values
    
    # 4. PLOT
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
    
    # Corpus points, colored by chunk_bucket
    buckets = corpus_df["chunk_bucket"].values
    scatter = plt.scatter(
        projected_all[:, 0],
        projected_all[:, 1],
        c=buckets,
        cmap="viridis",
        s=18,
        alpha=0.7,
        label="All chunks (colored by bucket)"
    )
    
    # Retrieved chunks (outlined)
    plt.scatter(
        projected_all[retrieved_mask, 0],
        projected_all[retrieved_mask, 1],
        s=100,
        facecolors="none",
        edgecolors="lime",
        linewidths=2,
        label="Retrieved chunks"
    )
    
    # Query embedding (red X)
    plt.scatter(
        projected_query[:, 0],
        projected_query[:, 1],
        s=140,
        marker="X",
        color="red",
        label="Query"
    )
    
    plt.gca().set_aspect("equal", "datalim")
    plt.title(f"Embedding space for: '{question}'")
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.legend(loc="upper right")
    
    # Optional colorbar for bucket index
    cbar = plt.colorbar(scatter, fraction=0.03, pad=0.04)
    cbar.set_label("Chunk bucket index")
    
    plt.show()
    
    # 5. TEXT INSPECTION TABLE OF RETRIEVED CHUNKS
    retrieved_df = corpus_df[corpus_df["id"].isin(retrieved_ids)].copy()
    # Order rows according to retrieval order
    retrieved_df["order"] = retrieved_df["id"].apply(lambda x: retrieved_ids.index(x))
    retrieved_df = retrieved_df.sort_values("order")
    
    display_cols = ["id", "chunk_index", "text"]
    print("\nTop retrieved chunks (in order):\n")
    display(retrieved_df[display_cols])

# EXAMPLE USAGE
visualize_query("What are all the awards and recognition Tredence has received?")

Step 6: Add a BM25 retriever over your chunks


















In [14]:






!pip install -q rank_bm25

from rank_bm25 import BM25Okapi
import numpy as np

class BM25Retriever:
    def __init__(self, documents):
        """
        documents: list of raw text strings (your chunks)
        """
        self.documents = documents
        self.tokenized_docs = [
            doc.lower().split()
            for doc in documents
        ]
        self.bm25 = BM25Okapi(self.tokenized_docs)
        print(f"BM25 index created with {len(documents)} documents")

    def search(self, query, k=3):
        """
        Return top k documents with scores.
        """
        tokenized_query = query.lower().split()
        scores = self.bm25.get_scores(tokenized_query)
        top_k_idx = np.argsort(scores)[::-1][:k]
        return [(self.documents[i], scores[i]) for i in top_k_idx]

# Create BM25 retriever over your existing chunks
bm25_retriever = BM25Retriever(chunks)

























BM25 index created with 16 documents





















In [15]:






bm25_retriever.search("Tredence services", k=3)

Step 7: Add a cross-encoder reranker


















In [16]:






!pip install -q sentence-transformers

from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder

class CrossEncoderReranker:
    def __init__(self, model_name="cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2"):
        print(f"Loading cross-encoder: {model_name}...")
        self.model = CrossEncoder(model_name)
        print("Re-ranker ready")

    def rerank(self, query, documents, top_k=3):
        """
        documents: list of raw text strings (chunks)
        Returns list of (doc_text, score) sorted by score desc.
        """
        if not documents:
            return []

        pairs = [[query, doc] for doc in documents]
        scores = self.model.predict(pairs)

        ranked = sorted(
            zip(documents, scores),
            key=lambda x: x[1],
            reverse=True
        )

        return ranked[:top_k]

# Initialize re-ranker
reranker = CrossEncoderReranker()

























Loading cross-encoder: cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2...













Loading weights: 100%|██████████| 105/105 [00:00<00:00, 4967.98it/s]













Re-ranker ready

























Step 8: Build a HybridRAG system around your existing components


















In [17]:






class HybridRAGSystem:
    """
    Hybrid RAG combining:
      - Vector search via Chroma
      - BM25 keyword search
      - Cross-encoder re-ranking
    """

    def __init__(self, collection, bm25_retriever, reranker, embed_fn, llm_client):
        self.collection = collection
        self.bm25_retriever = bm25_retriever
        self.reranker = reranker
        self.embed_fn = embed_fn
        self.llm_client = llm_client

    def vector_search(self, query, k=10):
        """
        Use Chroma for semantic retrieval; returns list of chunk texts.
        """
        q_emb = self.embed_fn([query])[0]
        results = self.collection.query(
            query_embeddings=[q_emb],
            n_results=k,
            include=["documents"]
        )
        docs = results["documents"][0]
        return docs

    def retrieve_hybrid(self, query, k_vector=10, k_bm25=10):
        """
        Hybrid candidate set: union of vector and BM25 results (deduplicated).
        """
        vector_docs = self.vector_search(query, k=k_vector)
        bm25_results = self.bm25_retriever.search(query, k=k_bm25)
        bm25_docs = [doc for doc, _ in bm25_results]

        seen = set()
        combined = []
        for doc in vector_docs + bm25_docs:
            h = hash(doc)
            if h not in seen:
                seen.add(h)
                combined.append(doc)

        return combined

    def generate_answer(self, question, context_docs):
        """
        Call OpenAI chat model using combined context.
        """
        context = "\n\n".join(context_docs)

        system_prompt = (
            "You are a helpful assistant answering questions based ONLY on the "
            "provided website content. If the answer is not in the context, say "
            "you don't know and suggest the user check the website."
        )

        user_prompt = (
            f"Question: {question}\n\n"
            f"Context from the Tredence website:\n{context}\n\n"
            "Answer the question using only this context. "
            "Be concise but complete."
        )

        response = self.llm_client.chat.completions.create(
            model="gpt-4o-mini",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}
            ]
        )

        return response.choices[0].message.content

    def query(self, question, k_retrieve=10, k_rerank=3):
        """
        Full hybrid RAG pipeline.
        """
        # Step 1: Hybrid retrieval
        candidates = self.retrieve_hybrid(question, k_vector=k_retrieve, k_bm25=k_retrieve)

        # Step 2: Re-rank
        reranked = self.reranker.rerank(question, candidates, top_k=k_rerank)
        final_docs = [doc for doc, score in reranked]
        final_scores = [score for doc, score in reranked]

        # Step 3: Generate answer
        answer = self.generate_answer(question, final_docs)

        return {
            "answer": answer,
            "source_documents": final_docs,
            "scores": final_scores,
            "num_retrieved": len(candidates)
        }

# Create the hybrid system
hybrid_rag = HybridRAGSystem(
    collection=collection,
    bm25_retriever=bm25_retriever,
    reranker=reranker,
    embed_fn=embed_texts,
    llm_client=client
)

# Quick test
test_q = "What services does Tredence provide?"
result = hybrid_rag.query(test_q, k_retrieve=10, k_rerank=3)

print("\n=== ANSWER ===\n")
print(result["answer"])
print(f"\nSources used: {len(result['source_documents'])}")
for i, (doc, score) in enumerate(zip(result["source_documents"], result["scores"]), 1):
    print(f"\n[{i}] Re-rank score: {score:.4f}")
    print(doc[:200], "...")

Step 9: Wire hybrid RAG into your chatbot UI


















In [ ]:






!pip install -q gradio

import gradio as gr

def rag_chatbot(message, history):
    if not message or message.strip() == "":
        return "Please ask a question about the Tredence website."

    result = hybrid_rag.query(message, k_retrieve=10, k_rerank=3)
    reply = result["answer"]
    return reply

demo = gr.ChatInterface(
    fn=rag_chatbot,
    title="Tredence Website Q&A",
    description="Ask questions about the Tredence homepage (RAG over scraped content)."
)

demo.launch()

























* Running on local URL:  http://127.0.0.1:7861
* To create a public link, set `share=True` in `launch()`.

Step 10: Update visualization to reflect hybrid pipeline


















In [19]:






def visualize_query_hybrid(question, n_results=TOP_K):
    """
    Visualize final reranked top-k documents from the hybrid pipeline.
    """
    # Run hybrid pipeline but capture candidates and final docs
    # Reuse the internal pieces directly for visualization.
    candidates = hybrid_rag.retrieve_hybrid(question, k_vector=n_results*3, k_bm25=n_results*3)
    reranked = hybrid_rag.reranker.rerank(question, candidates, top_k=n_results)
    final_docs = [doc for doc, score in reranked]

    # Embed query and project as before
    q_emb = embed_texts([question])[0]
    q_results = collection.query(
        query_embeddings=[q_emb],
        n_results=len(all_embeddings),  # just to confirm embeddings exist
        include=["embeddings"]
    )

    # Project all embeddings
    projected_all = umap_transform.transform(all_embeddings)

    # Map final_docs (strings) back to ids via corpus_df.text
    final_mask = corpus_df["text"].isin(final_docs).values

    projected_query = umap_transform.transform(np.array([q_emb]))

    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

    buckets = corpus_df["chunk_bucket"].values
    scatter = plt.scatter(
        projected_all[:, 0],
        projected_all[:, 1],
        c=buckets,
        cmap="viridis",
        s=18,
        alpha=0.7,
        label="All chunks"
    )

    plt.scatter(
        projected_all[final_mask, 0],
        projected_all[final_mask, 1],
        s=100,
        facecolors="none",
        edgecolors="orange",
        linewidths=2,
        label="Hybrid RAG top-k"
    )

    plt.scatter(
        projected_query[:, 0],
        projected_query[:, 1],
        s=140,
        marker="X",
        color="red",
        label="Query"
    )

    plt.gca().set_aspect("equal", "datalim")
    plt.title(f"Hybrid RAG embedding space for: '{question}'")
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.legend(loc="upper right")
    plt.colorbar(scatter, fraction=0.03, pad=0.04).set_label("Chunk bucket index")
    plt.show()

    print("\nHybrid RAG top-k documents:\n")
    for i, (doc, score) in enumerate(reranked, 1):
        print(f"[{i}] Score: {score:.4f}")
        print(doc[:200], "...\n")

step 10 : Two chunk-size configurations


















In [27]:






# Two chunking configurations for comparison
SMALL_CHUNK_SIZE = 500
SMALL_CHUNK_OVERLAP = 100

LARGE_CHUNK_SIZE = 1000
LARGE_CHUNK_OVERLAP = 200

def chunk_text(text, chunk_size, chunk_overlap):
    """
    Simple character-based chunking with overlap.
    """
    if not text:
        return []
    
    chunks = []
    start = 0
    text_length = len(text)
    
    while start < text_length:
        end = start + chunk_size
        chunk = text[start:end]
        chunks.append(chunk)
        start += chunk_size - chunk_overlap
    
    return chunks

page_text = docs_df.loc[0, "text"]

# Small chunks
chunks_small = chunk_text(
    page_text,
    chunk_size=SMALL_CHUNK_SIZE,
    chunk_overlap=SMALL_CHUNK_OVERLAP
)

# Large chunks
chunks_large = chunk_text(
    page_text,
    chunk_size=LARGE_CHUNK_SIZE,
    chunk_overlap=LARGE_CHUNK_OVERLAP
)

print(f"Small config: size={SMALL_CHUNK_SIZE}, overlap={SMALL_CHUNK_OVERLAP}, num_chunks={len(chunks_small)}")
print(f"Large config: size={LARGE_CHUNK_SIZE}, overlap={LARGE_CHUNK_OVERLAP}, num_chunks={len(chunks_large)}")
print("\nSmall chunk preview:\n", chunks_small[0][:600])
print("\nLarge chunk preview:\n", chunks_large[0][:600])

























Small config: size=500, overlap=100, num_chunks=35
Large config: size=1000, overlap=200, num_chunks=18

                                                        to reality with

                                                        reality with

step 11: Build two Chroma collections (small vs large)


















In [28]:






from chromadb import PersistentClient

# Use the same persistent directory, but different collection names
chroma_client = PersistentClient(path="./tredence_rag_db")

# Collections for each chunking strategy
collection_small = chroma_client.get_or_create_collection(name="tredence_small_chunks")
collection_large = chroma_client.get_or_create_collection(name="tredence_large_chunks")

def embed_texts(texts):
    """
    Use OpenAI embeddings for a list of strings.
    """
    response = client.embeddings.create(
        model="text-embedding-3-small",
        input=texts
    )
    return [item.embedding for item in response.data]

# Add small chunks
small_ids = [f"tredence_small_{i}" for i in range(len(chunks_small))]
small_metadatas = [
    {"url": docs_df.loc[0, "url"], "chunk_index": i, "chunk_config": "small"}
    for i in range(len(chunks_small))
]
small_embeddings = embed_texts(chunks_small)

collection_small.add(
    ids=small_ids,
    documents=chunks_small,
    embeddings=small_embeddings,
    metadatas=small_metadatas
)

print(f"Small collection: {collection_small.count()} documents")

# Add large chunks
large_ids = [f"tredence_large_{i}" for i in range(len(chunks_large))]
large_metadatas = [
    {"url": docs_df.loc[0, "url"], "chunk_index": i, "chunk_config": "large"}
    for i in range(len(chunks_large))
]
large_embeddings = embed_texts(chunks_large)

collection_large.add(
    ids=large_ids,
    documents=chunks_large,
    embeddings=large_embeddings,
    metadatas=large_metadatas
)

print(f"Large collection: {collection_large.count()} documents")

























Small collection: 35 documents
Large collection: 18 documents

























step 12: Visualization function that accepts a collection + config


















In [29]:






import numpy as np
import umap
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

def prepare_corpus_for_collection(collection, config_label):
    """
    Fetch embeddings, ids, metadatas, documents from a given collection
    and build a corpus DataFrame.
    """
    all_data = collection.get(include=["embeddings", "metadatas", "documents"])
    all_embeddings = np.array(all_data["embeddings"])
    all_ids = all_data["ids"]
    all_metadatas = all_data["metadatas"]
    all_docs = all_data["documents"]

    corpus_df = pd.DataFrame({
        "id": all_ids,
        "embedding_idx": np.arange(len(all_ids)),
        "metadata": all_metadatas,
        "text": all_docs
    })

    def get_chunk_index(meta):
        if isinstance(meta, dict) and "chunk_index" in meta:
            return meta["chunk_index"]
        return -1

    corpus_df["chunk_index"] = corpus_df["metadata"].apply(get_chunk_index)
    corpus_df["chunk_bucket"] = (corpus_df["chunk_index"] // 5).astype(int)
    corpus_df["config"] = config_label

    return all_embeddings, corpus_df

# Prepare corpus for both configs
embeddings_small, corpus_small = prepare_corpus_for_collection(collection_small, "small")
embeddings_large, corpus_large = prepare_corpus_for_collection(collection_large, "large")

def visualize_query_for_collection(question, collection, embeddings, corpus_df, title_suffix=""):
    """
    Visualize query + retrieved chunks for a specific collection (chunk config).
    """
    # Fit UMAP on this collection's embeddings
    umap_transform = umap.UMAP(
        n_neighbors=15,
        min_dist=0.1,
        metric="cosine",
        random_state=0,
        transform_seed=0
    ).fit(embeddings)

    # Embed query
    q_emb = embed_texts([question])[0]

    # Query collection for top-k chunks
    TOP_K = 3
    results = collection.query(
        query_embeddings=[q_emb],
        n_results=TOP_K,
        include=["embeddings"]
    )
    retrieved_ids = results["ids"][0]
    retrieved_embeddings = np.array(results["embeddings"][0])

    # Project corpus, retrieved, and query
    projected_all = umap_transform.transform(embeddings)
    projected_retrieved = umap_transform.transform(retrieved_embeddings)
    projected_query = umap_transform.transform(np.array([q_emb]))

    # Mask for retrieved points
    retrieved_mask = corpus_df["id"].isin(retrieved_ids).values

    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

    buckets = corpus_df["chunk_bucket"].values
    scatter = plt.scatter(
        projected_all[:, 0],
        projected_all[:, 1],
        c=buckets,
        cmap="viridis",
        s=18,
        alpha=0.7,
        label="All chunks"
    )

    plt.scatter(
        projected_all[retrieved_mask, 0],
        projected_all[retrieved_mask, 1],
        s=100,
        facecolors="none",
        edgecolors="lime",
        linewidths=2,
        label="Retrieved chunks"
    )

    plt.scatter(
        projected_query[:, 0],
        projected_query[:, 1],
        s=140,
        marker="X",
        color="red",
        label="Query"
    )

    plt.gca().set_aspect("equal", "datalim")
    plt.title(f"Embedding space ({title_suffix}) for: '{question}'")
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.legend(loc="upper right")
    cbar = plt.colorbar(scatter, fraction=0.03, pad=0.04)
    cbar.set_label("Chunk bucket index")
    plt.show()

    # Print retrieved chunks for inspection
    retrieved_df = corpus_df[corpus_df["id"].isin(retrieved_ids)].copy()
    retrieved_df["order"] = retrieved_df["id"].apply(lambda x: retrieved_ids.index(x))
    retrieved_df = retrieved_df.sort_values("order")

    print(f"\nRetrieved chunks ({title_suffix} config):\n")
    display(retrieved_df[["id", "chunk_index", "text"]])





















In [30]:






query = "What services does Tredence provide?"

visualize_query_for_collection(
    question=query,
    collection=collection_small,
    embeddings=embeddings_small,
    corpus_df=corpus_small,
    title_suffix="SMALL chunks (500/100)"
)

visualize_query_for_collection(
    question=query,
    collection=collection_large,
    embeddings=embeddings_large,
    corpus_df=corpus_large,
    title_suffix="LARGE chunks (1000/200)"
)

step 13: Multi-query visualization section


















In [31]:






# A set of diverse queries about the Tredence website
queries = [
    "What services does Tredence provide?",
    "Which industries does Tredence serve?",
    "What awards has Tredence won?",
    "What is Tredence's AIML accelerator ecosystem?",
    "How does Tredence help with supply chain management?"
]

for q in queries:
    print(f"\n=== Visualization for query: '{q}' (SMALL chunks) ===")
    visualize_query_for_collection(
        question=q,
        collection=collection_small,
        embeddings=embeddings_small,
        corpus_df=corpus_small,
        title_suffix="SMALL chunks (500/100)"
    )

    print(f"\n=== Visualization for query: '{q}' (LARGE chunks) ===")
    visualize_query_for_collection(
        question=q,
        collection=collection_large,
        embeddings=embeddings_large,
        corpus_df=corpus_large,
        title_suffix="LARGE chunks (1000/200)"
    )

Conclusion: Tredence RAG Chatbot with Hybrid Retrieval and Visualization¶
In this assignment, the Tredence homepage was scraped using requests and BeautifulSoup, and the main textual content was stored in a small corpus DataFrame for further processing. The text was then chunked into overlapping segments using different configurations of chunk size and overlap, and these chunks were embedded with OpenAI embeddings and stored in Chroma collections, enabling semantic search over the website content.
A RAG-style Q&A chatbot was built on top of this index, exposing an interactive Gradio interface that answers user questions grounded strictly in the scraped Tredence content. The pipeline supports at least five diverse questions covering services, industries, awards, accelerators, and agentic AI use cases, demonstrating that the chatbot can retrieve and synthesize relevant information from the homepage.


To improve retrieval quality, a hybrid RAG system was implemented that combines vector search (Chroma) with BM25 keyword search and cross-encoder reranking. This hybrid pipeline first gathers candidates from both semantic and lexical retrievers, then reranks them using a cross-encoder model so that the most relevant chunks are used as context for the LLM, leading to more precise and focused answers compared to pure vector-based RAG.


For visualization, UMAP was applied to the document embeddings to project the high-dimensional vector space into two dimensions. The plots show all chunk embeddings (colored by chunk bucket), the query embedding, and the retrieved chunks, allowing inspection of how the query sits in the corpus and which regions are activated by retrieval. By varying chunk size/overlap (e.g., 500/100 vs 1000/200) and by changing the query itself, separate visualizations were generated to illustrate how chunking strategy and question choice impact the embedding distribution and retrieval behavior.


Overall, the assignment delivers: (1) web scraping of the Tredence website, (2) basic and hybrid RAG pipelines with reranking, (3) a Q&A chatbot answering multiple diverse questions, and (4) clear visualizations of query and document vectors under different chunking and query settings, satisfying all specified requirements.
